project_items.title: RRBS/WGBS HPC Genomic ETL Pipelines
project_items.summary: End-to-end genomic processing workflows on HPC infrastructure built for scale, reproducibility, and downstream-ready biological analysis.
project_items.semantic_text: HPC workflow engineering for RRBS and WGBS genomic ETL. This project turns raw sequencing reads into analysis-ready outputs through scalable, reproducible pipelines using Python, Bash, and SLURM. The focus is large-sample execution, data transformation, QC, and reliable downstream handoff.

project_items.title: DNA Methylation Age Clock Modeling
project_items.summary: An engineering-focused extension of an existing lab workflow into a more configurable and reproducible system for methylation-based age prediction and experimentation.
project_items.semantic_text: Reproducible modeling and workflow engineering for DNA methylation age prediction. This project turns a narrower lab method into a configurable experimentation system with elastic-net modeling, multiple validation modes, structured outputs, metadata capture, and cleaner auditability.

project_items.title: Self-Hosted Bioinformatics AI / Internal RAG System
project_items.summary: A local research assistant built around retrieval-augmented generation for curated scientific content, with a focus on internal use rather than generic chatbot behavior.
project_items.semantic_text: Local-first AI tooling for scientific environments. This project uses document ingestion, chunking, sentence-transformer embeddings, FAISS indexing, and a Streamlit interface to support retrieval over curated scientific corpora for internal research use.

concept_cards.title: HPC Workflow Engineering
concept_cards.summary: Parallel research workflows built for scale, scheduling, and repeatability.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card highlights engineering work where orchestration, throughput, and reliable execution matter more than one-off scripting.
concept_cards.semantic_text: HPC workflow engineering for research environments. Parallel pipelines, scheduled execution, reproducibility, SLURM orchestration, and throughput-focused systems design.

concept_cards.title: Genomic Data Transformation
concept_cards.summary: Turning sequencing outputs into clean downstream-ready data products.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card emphasizes the transformation layer between raw biological inputs and usable analysis artifacts.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Genomic data transformation for sequencing workflows. Converting raw reads and intermediate outputs into structured, quality-controlled data products for downstream biological analysis and machine learning.

concept_cards.title: Scientific ETL
concept_cards.summary: Structured ingestion, cleaning, validation, and export for research datasets.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card captures practical data engineering in scientific settings, where inputs are messy and downstream users need trustable outputs.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Scientific ETL for research data systems. Ingestion, cleaning, validation, chunking, transformation, and export into forms that support analysis, modeling, or retrieval.

concept_cards.title: Batch Processing at Scale
concept_cards.summary: Managing large sample volumes and long-running jobs reliably.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card points to systems that need to hold up under real volume rather than a handful of test cases.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Batch processing at scale for scientific workflows. Large sample counts, long-running jobs, reliable execution, and practical handling of throughput and resource constraints.

concept_cards.title: Reproducible Modeling
concept_cards.summary: Modeling workflows designed to be rerun, compared, and audited.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card focuses on the engineering discipline around ML experiments rather than just getting one model run to work.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Reproducible modeling workflows for research ML. Systems built so runs can be repeated, compared, logged, and audited cleanly over time.

concept_cards.title: Methylation Modeling
concept_cards.summary: Engineering around methylation-based prediction and analysis workflows.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card highlights domain-specific modeling work tied directly to biological data rather than generic ML examples.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Methylation-focused modeling for biological prediction. Engineering systems for methylation age clock experiments, structured outputs, and repeatable validation.

concept_cards.title: Feature Engineering for Biology
concept_cards.summary: Converting biological signals into model-ready representations.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card connects biological preprocessing with the modeling side of the work.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Feature engineering for biology and genomics. Taking biological measurements or sequencing-derived outputs and preparing them for modeling, scoring, and downstream analysis.

concept_cards.title: Cross-Validation Design
concept_cards.summary: Building evaluation workflows that are configurable and statistically defensible.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card highlights the mechanics of designing better experiment evaluation, not just training a model once.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Cross-validation design for reproducible model evaluation. Flexible modes, repeat-aware experiments, and defensible workflows for comparing model behavior.

concept_cards.title: Experiment Logging
concept_cards.summary: Capturing runs, parameters, outputs, and comparisons cleanly.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card is about making experiments traceable instead of opaque.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Experiment logging and metadata capture for research ML. Recording run setup, outputs, parameters, and comparisons to support auditability and clearer iteration.

concept_cards.title: Configuration-Driven Pipelines
concept_cards.summary: Tools that behave predictably because inputs and settings are explicit.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card points to engineering work that reduces hidden assumptions and hardcoded execution paths.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Configuration-driven pipelines and workflows. Explicit parameters, controlled execution, and tools that are easier to rerun, share, and extend.

concept_cards.title: Data Quality and Validation
concept_cards.summary: Catching inconsistencies, anomalies, and malformed inputs early.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card represents the guardrail layer that keeps downstream results from drifting into garbage.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Data quality and validation for scientific workflows. Checking inputs, outputs, structure, and anomalies early so downstream analysis stays reliable.

concept_cards.title: Scientific Software Reliability
concept_cards.summary: Building tools that are useful beyond one-off scripts.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card highlights the difference between exploratory code and software that other people can actually rely on.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Scientific software reliability in research environments. Building tools that move beyond ad hoc scripts toward repeatable, maintainable, useful systems.

concept_cards.title: Research Computing Systems
concept_cards.summary: Engineering for real lab environments, not just toy notebooks.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card is the broad umbrella for systems work in scientific settings where operational reality matters.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Research computing systems built for scientific environments. Work that combines biological data, robust engineering, reproducibility, and practical usability.

concept_cards.title: Retrieval for Research
concept_cards.summary: Using search and ranking to surface useful scientific knowledge fast.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card highlights retrieval as a practical research tool instead of generic chat novelty.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Retrieval for research settings. Search, ranking, and knowledge access systems that help researchers find the right scientific information quickly.

concept_cards.title: Semantic Search Workflows
concept_cards.summary: Embeddings, similarity, and ranked retrieval over curated content.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card shows the more ML-specific retrieval layer in a way that is concrete and compact.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Semantic search workflows with embeddings and vector similarity. Curated scientific content, sentence-transformers, FAISS, and ranked retrieval.

concept_cards.title: Local-First AI Tools
concept_cards.summary: AI systems designed around privacy, control, and local corpora.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card reflects the design choice to keep scientific workflows controlled and practical rather than outsourced to generic external tools.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Local-first AI tools for controlled scientific environments. Retrieval over local corpora, privacy-conscious design, and practical internal use.

concept_cards.title: Internal Research Assistants
concept_cards.summary: Lab-facing tools that help researchers query and navigate knowledge.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card frames AI tooling as a practical assistant for internal research work.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Internal research assistants for scientific knowledge work. Lab-facing tools that help users query curated content and navigate research information more effectively.

concept_cards.title: Compact Model Workflows
concept_cards.summary: Interest in efficient models that are practical to run and ship.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card gives you a place to connect future compact-model work to the semantic explorer without pretending you have already built the whole thing.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Compact model workflows and efficient AI. Interest in smaller practical models that are easier to run, ship, and integrate into useful applications.

concept_cards.title: On-Device AI
concept_cards.summary: Applied interest in edge-friendly inference and lightweight deployment.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card lets the site surface your current direction even before it becomes a full flagship project.
concept_cards.semantic_text: On-device AI and edge-friendly inference. Interest in lightweight deployment, efficient models, and practical local execution.

concept_cards.title: Agentic Prototyping
concept_cards.summary: Building ad hoc apps quickly with coding agents and structured workflows.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card reflects your current personal builder mode and points to how you like to work.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Agentic prototyping with coding agents. Building ad hoc applications quickly using tools like Codex and Claude, with an eye toward practical workflows and experimentation.

concept_cards.title: CLI and Developer Tooling
concept_cards.summary: Practical command-line tools that make repeated work faster.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card captures the developer ergonomics side of your work.
concept_cards.semantic_text: CLI and developer tooling for repeated technical work. Tools that improve speed, reduce friction, and make workflows more usable over time.

concept_cards.title: Interactive Research Apps
concept_cards.summary: Small usable interfaces for technical workflows and data exploration.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card gives space to the UI side of the work without overstating full product engineering.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Interactive research apps and lightweight technical interfaces. Small applications that expose useful scientific or AI logic in a usable way.

concept_cards.title: Scientific Data Storytelling
concept_cards.summary: Making complex technical outputs understandable and actionable.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card covers the communication layer between raw computation and human use.
concept_cards.semantic_text: Scientific data storytelling through reporting, visualization, and interpretation. Turning technical outputs into forms people can actually use.

concept_cards.title: From Research to Engineering
concept_cards.summary: Taking research logic and hardening it into real tooling.
concept_cards.why_it_matters: This card is a clean high-level theme that connects several of your strongest projects.
concept_cards.semantic_text: From research to engineering. Taking narrower scientific logic and turning it into more robust, reusable, scalable tooling for real environments.
